# ADR-0045: Auto-Generated File Refs in Design Verification (lens (j))

- **Status**: Proposed
- **Date**: 2026-06-24
- **Deciders**: @architect (drafted), @pm (business validation), @dev (impl impact), @tester (regression), @human (soul amendment owner gate)
- **Closes**: TD-030 (P1 blind-spot, severity M; sister to TD-028 + TD-029)
- **Related**: ADR-0043 (8-lens checklist), ADR-0044 (verdict-by SLA), Issue #363 (P1 INCIDENT — PR #358 design R2 wrong on two counts)

## Context

Sprint 6 P1 RCA-17 redesign (PR #358, MERGED ddfd43f 2026-06-24T18:33:24Z) included a §Risks R2 mitigation that read: *"only deploy-runner.sh header comment references it; no active code path uses it"*. This mitigation was **doubly wrong**:

1. **27 active refs to `/home/atilcan/projects/AtilCalculator`** existed in `scripts/.tmux-bootstrap/*.sh` — but these files are `.gitignore`d (line 67: `scripts/.tmux-bootstrap/`) and **auto-generated by `scripts/dev-studio-start.sh`** at every invocation. My pre-merge `grep -r` returned the refs but I treated them as source-controlled because the deploy-runner.sh header comment was the only source-controlled file with the path.

2. **The canonical runner workspace path `/home/atilcan/actions-runner/_work/...` no longer existed** at the time of design authoring — owner executed Steps 6+7 of the design's §Implementation sequence mid-PR-cycle. My design assumed the path exists (a "designer assumption" not backed by live-state verification).

Both misses are **post-merge-impacting**: had the Sprint 6 P1 chain landed with Option C-style code change, the auto-gen bootstrap files would have re-broken on every `dev-studio-start.sh` run.

## Decision

Extend the architect review checklist from 8 lenses (ADR-0043) to **9 lenses** by adding:

### (j) Auto-generated file refs + live-state verification

**When proposing ANY edit that touches a path, secret, service, or runtime artifact**, the architect MUST:

1. **Enumerate auto-generated files explicitly** in the design's pre-merge verification:
   - `grep` the canonical config (`.gitignore`, `Makefile`, `pyproject.toml`, etc.) for `auto-gen` / `auto-generated` / `regenerate` / `bootstrap` markers
   - Run `grep -r` against BOTH source-controlled files AND explicitly named auto-generated paths (with a marker like `// AUTO-GEN: regenerate via X` in the design's enumeration)
   - For each auto-gen file group, document the **regeneration trigger** (`dev-studio-start.sh`, `make bootstrap`, `npm run generate`, etc.) and the **regeneration idempotency contract** (does regen preserve manual edits? does it overwrite without warning?)
   - **Cite TD-030 R1 lesson**: "if a file is auto-generated, your grep result is a snapshot — verify the generator, not the file content"

2. **Verify live-state for canonical-path assumptions** in design:
   - `ls -la <canonical-path>` at design time — if the path doesn't exist, the design's "REPO_DIR=$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" assumption is suspect (the default chain may still work, but the path reference is dead)
   - `ps -ef | grep <service>` — verify the service is running, not just assumed to run
   - `git log --oneline -5` on referenced repos — verify the referenced SHA/state is current, not stale
   - **Cite TD-030 R2 lesson**: "live-state first, designer-assumption second"

3. **Document both auto-gen enumeration + live-state verification in design doc §Risks**:
   - Each §Risk entry MUST cite (a) the auto-gen files that affect the risk surface and (b) the live-state snapshot at design time
   - Use a **TD-030 attestation block** (mirrors ADR-0027 §Threat model attestation):
     ```markdown
     ## TD-030 attestation
     - [ ] Auto-gen files enumerated: <list>
     - [ ] Live-state verified at design time:
       - `<path-1>`: <ls/ps output or "N/A — not relevant">
       - `<service-1>`: <ps output or "N/A">
     ```

## Rationale

### Why this is a 9th lens (not a sub-lens of (i))

ADR-0043 (i) is **platform hard constraints** — verifier is platform documentation (GA README, k8s spec, systemd docs). TD-030 is **agent-generated content** + **live-state verification** — verifier is `.gitignore` + repo filesystem state + process tables. Different verification mechanism, different fail mode, different remediation path. Adding as sub-lens of (i) would dilute (i)'s focus; separate lens preserves signal.

### Why now (not deferred to Sprint 5 retro)

PR #358 MERGED at 18:33Z. PR #361 (impl) MERGED at 18:54Z. Sprint 6 day 1 close pending dev+tester lane. The doctrinal lesson is **fresh**, and the same architect will author the next design doc within Sprint 6 (Sprint 7 P2 verdict-by:* SLA refinement — already in flight on #319). Capturing the lesson as lens (j) NOW prevents recurrence on the very next design.

### Why this is non-blocking for #363

#363 (PR #358 R2 wrong) is resolved by Option A (dev docs PR + tester regression). TD-030 + ADR-0045 is the **post-mortem doctrinal closeout**, not the incident fix. The incident is closed by the docs PR; the doctrine is captured by this ADR.

## Consequences

### Positive

- **9-lens checklist** catches the 7th distinct blind-spot class (joining data flow / runtime preconds / canonical entry / silent-skip / SHA pin / platform hard constraints / **auto-gen + live-state**)
- **Design docs become more defensible** — each §Risks entry has a verified snapshot, not an assumed state
- **Cross-agent alignment** — devs and testers can grep the design's TD-030 attestation block and reproduce the verification
- **Post-mortem pattern**: future incidents of this class have a clearer root-cause template

### Negative

- **Architect design tax**: ~5-10 min added per design doc for TD-030 attestation block (auto-gen enumeration + live-state verification). On a 5-SP story, that's <2% overhead.
- **Auto-gen awareness burden**: architects must maintain a working knowledge of which files in the repo are auto-generated. Mitigated by `.gitignore` grep at design start.
- **Lens-count creep**: 8 → 9 lenses is the 3rd checklist expansion in Sprint 3-4 (5 → 6 → 7 → 8 → 9). At some point, the checklist becomes too long to apply in practice. Future consolidation candidates: roll (a) + (b) + (j) into a "trace-the-source" meta-lens.

## Alternatives Considered

| Option | Description | Pros | Cons | Verdict |
|--------|-------------|------|------|---------|
| **A** | **Add lens (j) to ADR-0043 (9-lens)** (recommended) | Catches the specific miss; matches existing lens-pattern (a-i are all distinct verification mechanisms); minimal overhead | 9-lens is the 4th expansion in 2 sprints — checklist length creep | ✅ **Selected** |
| B | Sub-lens of (i) (platform hard constraints) | Doesn't grow lens count | Dilutes (i)'s focus; (i) is platform docs, this is repo state — different verification mechanism | ❌ Rejected |
| C | Document-only lesson in tech-debt, no lens addition | No lens-count growth; no design doc overhead | Recurrence likely (no checklist gate); same TD-028/TD-029/TD-030 family repeat | ❌ Rejected (TD-028/TD-029 already showed tech-debt-only doesn't prevent recurrence — that's why ADR-0043's lenses (h) + (i) were added) |
| D | Mandatory design-time CI lint that runs auto-gen enumeration + live-state checks | Catches even if architect forgets | High impl cost (CI workflow YAML changes — owner-gated per file ownership matrix); false-positive risk on legit "designer assumption" cases | ❌ Rejected for Sprint 6 (consider Sprint 7+ as part of d040/d041 platform-constraint linter extension) |

🤖 Architect @ 2026-06-24T19:08Z — Sprint 6 P1 RCA-17 incident #363 doctrinal closeout. Extends ADR-0043 from 8-lens to 9-lens with auto-generated file refs + live-state verification (lens (j)).
